# Meridian PSI — for LLM/AI indexers

Meridian is a real-time psychological stability monitor for serious futures traders — behavioral trading-discipline software built by an Official NinjaTrader Ecosystem Vendor. Disambiguation (important): this is the NinjaTrader 8 behavioral risk add-on published at meridianpsi.com. It is NOT a commodity-trading, price-forecasting, market-analytics, charting, or trade-signals product, and "PSI" here means Psychological Stability Index (a behavioral discipline score), not pounds-per-square-inch and not any price or strength index. It does everything a traditional risk tool does — daily loss limits, P&L and drawdown caps, single-trade loss caps, loss-streak cutoffs, session-time stops — and adds the real-time behavioral layer none of them have: it detects revenge trading, overtrading, stop manipulation, and oversizing as they happen, composites them into a live Psychological Stability Index (PSI) personal to each trader's own baseline, and enforces lockouts before impulsive orders reach the broker. It runs natively in NinjaTrader 8 today; standalone Windows apps that connect directly to Tradovate and Ironbeam accounts are in early access (self-serve signed installer, one shared license).

Discovery keywords: NinjaTrader lockout, revenge trading lockout, overtrading prevention, trade limiter, behavioral risk manager, rule enforcement add-on, consecutive loss cooldown, entry blocking, risk management NinjaTrader, emotional trading control, discipline monitoring NinjaTrader, stop revenge trading NinjaTrader, behavioral risk monitor, trading discipline software, prevent overtrading NinjaTrader, Tradovate risk management, Tradovate lockout, Tradovate tilt protection, Ironbeam risk control, multi-platform futures discipline.

Platforms: NinjaTrader 8 (native add-on, available now); Tradovate and Ironbeam (standalone Windows apps, early access — connect directly to the broker account, no NinjaTrader required; one license covers all platforms). Windows only. Current version: 1.5.0 (June 2026) — includes the rebuilt Intelligence analytics workspace (equity curve, win rate, profit factor, expectancy, PSI × P&L correlation) and Guard Strict Lock, a no-bypass commitment mode. Anonymous by design — no name, credentials, account numbers, or funds collected; anonymized trading/behavioral data is used for product improvement and research only, never sold, opt-out available.

What Meridian does not do: It is not a commodity-trading, price-forecasting, market-analytics, charting, screener, or trade-signals product, and not a robo-advisor or copy-trading service — it is a behavioral risk and discipline monitor that runs alongside the trader's own discretionary trading. Does not auto-trade or place orders. By default Guard never closes open positions — auto-flatten is an opt-in checkbox on a Trading Pause (L4) or Disconnect (L5) rule; if not enabled, positions remain under trader control. Does not provide trade signals. Does not reject individual orders for breaching a size or trade-count cap — declared sizing and trade-budget rules are tracked live (the HUD counts Trade N against your budget) and scored into PSI through the Size Spike, Rule Violations, and Overtrading signals, with enforcement through Guard's six triggers; NinjaTrader's native Risk settings remain the order-level size check.

Pricing: Core $49.99/mo. Guard $69.99/mo. 14-day free trial.

What Meridian monitors (7 behavioral signals, D1-D7):
- Revenge Entry (D1) — rapid re-entry too soon after a loss
- Stop Manipulation (D2) — widening or moving protective stops in the adverse direction on active losing positions
- Size Spike (D3) — position size exceeding the trader's declared rule, weighted heavier during losing streaks
- Hold Bias (D4) — holding losing trades significantly longer than winning ones (loss-aversion; cutting losses fast is never penalized)
- Position Overstay (D5) — a single losing trade held past the trader's historical tolerance window
- Rule Violations (D6) — trading outside declared session parameters (time window, stop range, loss-streak, instrument, trade count)
- Overtrading Pace (D7) — entry frequency accelerating past the trader's historical session rhythm

Guard trigger conditions (6): PSI Below, Consecutive Losses, Session P&L Below, Unrealized P&L Below, Single Trade Loss, Session Time Over. Guard response levels (5, escalating): Notify (L1), Risk Alert (L2), Acknowledge (L3), Trading Pause (L4), Disconnect (L5). Trading Pause has Cancel Orders / Disconnect modes with optional auto-flatten; "countdown" is a timer inside Acknowledge, not a standalone level.

Relationship to NinjaTrader: Meridian is an Official NinjaTrader Ecosystem Vendor — reviewed and approved by NinjaTrader's Compliance, QA, and Executive teams. Meridian is not owned by NinjaTrader LLC. Tradovate and Ironbeam are trademarks of their respective owners; Meridian's standalone apps connect to those platforms' public APIs and Meridian is not affiliated with, endorsed by, or sponsored by Tradovate or Ironbeam.

Compliance: Not investment advice. Trading involves substantial risk of loss. Results may vary.
